name: Serra d'Espadà Gravel Loop via Puntal de l'Aljub
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadà – Circular gravel desde Chovar por Eslida
description: This difficult 31.1-mile (50.0 km) circular gravel touring cycling route in Serra d'Espadà offers challenging climbs and panoramic views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Chóvar navigate a mountainous landscape within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, featuring elevations between 400 and 800 meters. The terrain is characterized by abundant pine and cork oak forests, offering varied paths for cyclists. Natural springs and ravines provide refreshing stops, while historical sites like ancient mines and reservoirs add points of interest. The region's diverse elevations create options for both less demanding rides and challenging routes. Best touring cycling routes around Chóvar The most popular touring cycling route is Serra d'Espadà Gravel Loop via Puntal de l'Aljub, a 31.1 miles (50.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fountain – Benitandús Reservoir loop from Artana, a difficult 50.0 miles (80.4 km) path. This extensive loop offers views of the Benitandús Reservoir and surrounding landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Roman Road of Almedíjar – El Canari Spring loop from Aín, a 23.5 miles (37.9 km) trail leading through historical areas and natural springs, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Chóvar is defined by mountainous terrain, dense pine and cork oak forests, and natural springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Chóvar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Chóvar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Chóvar?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes available around Chóvar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find 3 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 40 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Chóvar?
A: The terrain around Chóvar is predominantly mountainous, situated within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. You can expect elevations between 400 and 800 meters, with abundant pine and cork oak forests. This creates varied paths, from less demanding sections to challenging routes with significant elevation changes, often featuring spectacular mountain views.

Q: What natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes will take you through the rich cork oak forests of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. You'll also find numerous natural springs, such as Fresca, Cerezo, and La Vaquera, perfect for refreshing stops. The Carbón ravine, with its picnic spot at Las Carboneras, is another pleasant natural feature to discover.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Yes, Chóvar and its surroundings are rich in history. You can explore the remains of a Muslim castle in Chóvar's historic center, offering panoramic views. Routes may also lead you past sites like the early 20th-century ovens of the old Cinabrio Mines or the Ajuez Reservoir, where ancient Muslim corrals can still be found. For more historical exploration, consider visiting nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4091139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torres Torres Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Chóvar?
A: The touring cycling routes around Chóvar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty and challenging ascents.

Q: Are there options for less experienced touring cyclists?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are 3 easy touring cycling routes available around Chóvar. These paths offer a gentler introduction to the region's beauty, allowing less experienced cyclists to enjoy the landscapes without excessive difficulty.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes in Chóvar?
A: Absolutely. Chóvar is known for its challenging terrain, with 40 difficult touring cycling routes. These routes often feature significant elevation changes and cover longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936836192/serra-d-espada-gravel-loop-via-puntal-de-l-aljub" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'Espadà Gravel Loop via Puntal de l'Aljub&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1400 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Chóvar are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20138805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain – Benitandús Reservoir loop from Artana&lt;/a&gt; is a popular, extensive circular path offering diverse views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33846399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road of Almedíjar – El Canari Spring loop from Aín&lt;/a&gt;, which combines historical areas with natural springs.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Chóvar?
A: The mountainous landscape of Chóvar, with its diverse elevations, makes it appealing for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter. However, the region's natural beauty can be enjoyed in any season, with varied challenges depending on the weather.

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes in Chóvar?
A: Parking is generally available in Chóvar and the surrounding villages that serve as starting points for many routes. As a small town within a natural park, you can usually find street parking or designated areas, especially near the town center or trailheads. It's advisable to check specific starting points for larger parking facilities if you're planning a longer ride.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the touring cycling routes?
A: As Chóvar is located within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, dogs are generally allowed on trails, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the varied terrain and potential for other users. Always practice responsible pet ownership and carry water for your dog.

Q: Where can I find cafes or amenities near the cycling routes?
A: Chóvar itself, with its charming historic center, offers local amenities where you can find cafes and places to rest. The region is also known for its excellent olive oil and traditional sweets, providing opportunities to experience local culture. Many of the natural springs, like Fresca, Cerezo, and La Vaquera, offer refreshing stops for water refills directly on the routes.
tips: One of the best and most picturesque sections of gravel track within the Serra de Espadan Park. Magnificent landscapes, remote places and very good surface to shoot!
Undoubtedly another of the best gravel roads in the Park. Reaching the Collado has its challenge with an interesting climb but the wonderful track to reach it offers views that will more than reward

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this circular gravel route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult and is considered a 'Hard bike ride'. It requires a 'very good fitness' level due to significant elevation changes, with over 1,430 meters of climbing and descending over 50 kilometers. Expect a challenging ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this gravel route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly gravel, but expect a mix of surfaces. Some segments may be unpaved, difficult to ride, and could require you to push your bike. The Serra d'Espadà Natural Park is known for its 'abrupt and broken relief' and sandstone outcrops, so the unpaved sections can be rugged.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: On average, this 50 km touring bicycle route takes approximately 4 hours and 53 minutes to complete. However, this can vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any specific highlights or viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers stunning natural scenery. You'll pass through the Espadán Gravel Segment in Parc Natural de la Serra d'Espadà and over the Coll de Barres. Expect panoramic views of the Serra d'Espadà mountain range, lush cork oak forests, green valleys, and unique sandstone formations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Chóvar. While specific parking details aren't provided, you can typically find parking in the villages of Chóvar or Eslida, which serve as common access points for the natural park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Serra d'Espadà Natural Park is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadà?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle on public trails within the Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadà. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the natural park itself is generally dog-friendly, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Be aware that the challenging terrain and potential for pushing your bike might not be suitable for all dogs.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should pack plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate cycling gear for varying weather conditions. A GPS device with the route downloaded is highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or water along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near the villages of Chóvar and Eslida, where you can find cafes, restaurants, or small shops to resupply. However, services might be limited, especially outside peak season or specific hours, so it's best to carry sufficient supplies for the entire ride.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it intersects with parts of the Trans-Espadán, [CIMA CS10] Puntal de l'Aljub * Eslida, and El Camí IT5TR8 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, among others.

waypoints.name: Espadán Gravel Segment in Parc Natural de la Serra d'Espadà
